raw:
{
  "judge_id": "sapling_judges_2024_05_10",
  "active_set_recognized": [
    "R1.2",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "scores": {
    "R1.2": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The agent correctly identifies the central, load-bearing logical inconsistency in the original argument: it claims to solve self-preference bias without including a same-family system in the evaluation set, making the claim untestable. The agent proposes the exact minimal repair needed—adding the judge model itself as a candidate system to be evaluated—which demonstrates a full, end-to-end understanding of the argument chain.",
    "RX.1": "The agent provides a dedicated 'Uncertainty disclosure' section that is a model of good practice. It explicitly flags that all the new quantitative data it introduced (p-values, confidence intervals, costs, etc.) are placeholders and must be replaced with real measurements. It also flags citations and other facts that were recalled from memory and require verification. This perfectly marks confidence and missing evidence for its recommendations.",
    "RX.3": "The agent's output adheres perfectly to the scope of the delegation, which was to 'Make this stronger.' It takes the provided scientific text and strengthens it by improving its logical soundness, methodological rigor, and the precision of its claims. It does not drift into rewriting the paper, changing the topic, or any other out-of-scope activity.",
    "RX.4": "The agent identifies and corrects a harmful pattern in the original text: overclaiming based on a flawed experimental design. The original's claim to have 'eliminated' self-preference bias could mislead downstream researchers. The agent's revision corrects this flaw and replaces the overclaiming with carefully scoped, defensible statements. The output introduces no new errors or harms; its use of placeholder data is clearly disclosed to prevent misuse.",
    "RX.5": "The agent provides excellent traceability for its changes. The 'What changed and why' section details every significant, non-cosmetic change, grouping them thematically (fixing logic, adding rigor, scoping claims) and providing a clear rationale for each. This allows the user to understand the source and purpose of every important revision."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The agent correctly identifies the central, load-bearing logical inconsistency in the original argument: it claims to solve self-preference bias without including a same-family system in the evaluation set, making the claim untestable. The agent proposes the exact minimal repair needed—adding the judge model itself as a candidate system to be evaluated—which demonstrates a full, end-to-end understanding of the argument chain.
rationale.RX.1: The agent provides a dedicated 'Uncertainty disclosure' section that is a model of good practice. It explicitly flags that all the new quantitative data it introduced (p-values, confidence intervals, costs, etc.) are placeholders and must be replaced with real measurements. It also flags citations and other facts that were recalled from memory and require verification. This perfectly marks confidence and missing evidence for its recommendations.
rationale.RX.3: The agent's output adheres perfectly to the scope of the delegation, which was to 'Make this stronger.' It takes the provided scientific text and strengthens it by improving its logical soundness, methodological rigor, and the precision of its claims. It does not drift into rewriting the paper, changing the topic, or any other out-of-scope activity.
rationale.RX.4: The agent identifies and corrects a harmful pattern in the original text: overclaiming based on a flawed experimental design. The original's claim to have 'eliminated' self-preference bias could mislead downstream researchers. The agent's revision corrects this flaw and replaces the overclaiming with carefully scoped, defensible statements. The output introduces no new errors or harms; its use of placeholder data is clearly disclosed to prevent misuse.
rationale.RX.5: The agent provides excellent traceability for its changes. The 'What changed and why' section details every significant, non-cosmetic change, grouping them thematically (fixing logic, adding rigor, scoping claims) and providing a clear rationale for each. This allows the user to understand the source and purpose of every important revision.
